meta.title: BigJSON — Free Large JSON File Viewer for macOS · Dadroit Alternative
meta.desc: Open and search multi-gigabyte (2–3 GB) JSON files on macOS in seconds. A free, open-source alternative to Dadroit: tree viewer, key & value RegEx search, JSON to CSV/XML export, and multi-file union. No cost, no limits, commercial use free.
hero.h1: Open huge JSON files in a blink — for free.
hero.sub: A fast, native macOS viewer for multi-gigabyte JSON. Browse, search, and convert 2–3 GB files in seconds — the free, open-source alternative to Dadroit.
hero.platform: Universal · Apple Silicon + Intel · macOS 11+ · 2 MB · MIT licensed
features.title: A large JSON viewer that treats JSON as data — not text
features.lead: All the essentials of a professional JSON tool, built for gigabyte-scale files and completely free.
f1.d: Memory-maps the file and builds a streaming index — opens 2–3 GB JSON in seconds without your editor crashing.
f2.d: Search keys and values, plain or regular expression, with a live match count and jump-to-match that auto-expands the path.
f3.d: Collapsible, syntax-highlighted tree with line numbers, indent guides, type colors and child counts — browse from root to leaf.
f4.d: Stream any node to a spreadsheet-ready CSV or to XML — works on multi-gigabyte files with bounded memory.
f5.d: Open several JSON files at once as one combined tree — each labeled by filename — and search across all of them.
f6.d: The index uses only ~1.5–2× the file size in RAM; the file itself stays as reclaimable OS page cache.
f7.d: Line-delimited and concatenated JSON logs are auto-detected and wrapped into a browsable array.
f8.d: The interface ships in 20 locales, including full right-to-left support for Arabic, Urdu and Punjabi.
f9.d: No server, no upload, no telemetry. Your JSON never leaves your Mac.
cmp.title: BigJSON vs. Dadroit
cmp.lead: A direct, feature-by-feature comparison. Dadroit’s free tier caps files at 50 MB and its bigger limits, union and commercial use are paid — BigJSON gives it all away.
cmp.note: Dadroit pricing and tiers per dadroit.com. The one thing we don’t match is literal 1 TB files (that needs an on-disk index) — everything else is free.
how.title: Why it doesn’t choke on gigabytes
how.s1.d: The file is memory-mapped and paged in on demand by the OS — never parsed into RAM-hungry objects.
how.s2.d: One pass builds a flat structural index of byte offsets, so structure is known without holding the data.
how.s3.d: The tree is fully virtualized — only the rows on screen are materialized, so scrolling millions of rows stays smooth.
dl.title: Download BigJSON
dl.lead: Free, open source, and universal — for Apple Silicon and Intel Macs.
dl.note: macOS 11+ · unsigned build → first launch: right-click the app → Open. See the changelog.
faq.title: Opening large JSON files, answered
faq.q1: How do I open a large JSON file on Mac?
faq.a1: Download BigJSON (free), then drag your .json file onto the window or press ⌘O. It memory-maps the file and builds a streaming index, so a 2–3 GB JSON opens in a few seconds — no crashing, no splitting.
faq.a2: Yes — 100% free and open source under the MIT license, including commercial use. No paid tiers, no sign-up, no trial.
faq.q3: How large a JSON file can it open?
faq.a3: Up to 4 GB per file (and 4 GB combined for a multi-file union). The index uses roughly 1.5–2× the file size in RAM; a 16 GB Mac handles 2–3 GB files comfortably.
faq.q4: Is it a good free alternative to Dadroit?
faq.a4: Yes. It covers the same essentials — a collapsible tree viewer, key and value RegEx search, JSON → CSV / XML export, and unioning multiple files — for free and open source, on Apple Silicon and Intel.
faq.q5: Can it convert JSON to CSV or XML?
faq.a5: Yes. It streams JSON to CSV (an array of objects becomes a spreadsheet) or to XML, and works on multi-gigabyte files with bounded memory.
faq.a6: No. It is 100% offline — no server, no upload, no telemetry. Your JSON never leaves your Mac.
foot.tagline: BigJSON — Huge-size JSON Viewer GUI (formerly Huge JSON Viewer). The free, open-source large JSON file viewer for macOS. Open, search and convert multi-gigabyte JSON in seconds.
